info.title: Portfolio Dividend Tracker (Staging)
info.description:
REST API for Portfolio Dividend Tracker. Authenticate using **OAuth2 Authorization Code with PKCE**. See the Authentication endpoints for full details.

> **LLM Agents:** fetch `/docs/api.json` for the machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification. To dynamically create an OAuth client, use the MCP discovery protocol: fetch `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` to discover the `registration_endpoint`, then `POST` to it with `client_name` and `redirect_uris` to receive a `client_id` for the OAuth2 PKCE flow.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: OAuth2 Authorization Code with PKCE. Use these endpoints to obtain and refresh access tokens for the API.

tags.name: Changelog
tags.description:
## v0.3.4 (2026-03-21)

## Added

- `GET /v1/portfolio/holdings` — Retrieve portfolio holdings without the portfolio summary

---

## v0.3.3 (2026-03-20)

## Added

- `GET /v1/me` — Get the authenticated user's profile (name, email, locale, timezone, currency, account status)
- `GET /v1/authorizations/{clientId}` — Get authorization details for an OAuth client with individual token information
- `GET /v1/imports` — List import history with status and result counts
- `GET /v1/imports/{importId}` — Get the status and results of an import (poll after creating)
- `POST /v1/imports` — Upload a broker export file to import transactions, dividends, bookings, and expenses (async, supports CSV, XLSX, TXT, XML with auto-detection)

## Changed

- API rate limit reduced from 10,000 to 250 requests per minute
- OAuth authorization screen is now translated based on the user's locale (English and Dutch)
- OAuth scope descriptions are translated at render time to match the user's language

---

## v0.3.2 (2026-03-20)

## Added

- `GET /v1/authorizations` — List OAuth applications that have been granted access to the account, grouped by client
- `DELETE /v1/authorizations/{clientId}` — Revoke all access and refresh tokens for an OAuth client
- `GET /v1/authorizations/{clientId}` — Get authorization details for an OAuth client with individual token information
- `DELETE /v1/authorizations/{clientId}/tokens/{tokenId}` — Revoke a single access token and its refresh token
- `GET /v1/me` — Get the authenticated user's profile (name, email, locale, timezone, currency, account status)

---

## v0.3.1 (2026-03-20)

## Added

- `GET /v1/corporate-actions` — Added `type` filter to narrow results by corporate action type (`stock_split`, `isin_change`, `exchange_change`)

---

## v0.3.0 (2026-03-20)

## Added

- `GET /v1/corporate-actions` — List corporate actions (stock splits, ISIN changes, exchange changes) with optional filtering by `symbolIds`, `symbolQuoteIds`, and date range (`dateFrom`, `dateTo`)

---

## v0.2.0 (2026-03-18)

## Added

- `GET /v1/portfolio/snapshots` — Added `dates` filter to retrieve snapshots for specific dates instead of a continuous range
- `GET /v1/portfolio/holdings/batch` — Batch get multiple portfolio holdings in a single request
- `GET /v1/prices` — List historical prices for a symbol quote (top-level endpoint)
- `GET /v1/prices/batch` — Batch list prices for multiple symbol quotes in a single request
- `GET /v1/changelog` — List API changelog entries
- `GET /v1/portfolio/gains` — Get total portfolio gains amount and percentage for a time period
- `GET /v1/portfolio/performance/chart` — Get portfolio performance as a time series with configurable fields and grouping (day, week, month, quarter, year)

## Changed

### Portfolio distributions

The `GET /v1/portfolio/distributions` response now includes `industries`, `industryGroups`, `continents`, `marketCapitalizations`, `exchanges`, `brokers`, and `strategies` distributions in addition to the existing `sectors`, `countries`, `currencies`, and `assetTypes`.

### Portfolio summary

The `GET /v1/portfolio` response now returns a detailed financial breakdown instead of the simplified summary. New fields include `unrealisedGainsProduct`, `unrealisedGainsExchangeRate`, `unrealisedGains`, `realisedGainsProduct`, `realisedGainsExchangeRate`, `realisedGains`, `totalGainsProduct`, `totalGainsExchangeRate`, `totalGains`, `totalBoughtPriceTotal`, `totalSoldPriceTotal`, `totalBoughtExpended`, `totalSoldReceived`, `totalBooked`, `totalExpended`, `transactionsCosts`, `transactionsTaxes`, `dividendsTax`, `dividendsReceivedWithoutTax`, and `dividendsGains`. The previous `totalValue`, `totalGainLoss`, `totalDividendsReceived`, and `cashPosition` fields have been replaced by `currentWorth`, `totalGains`, `dividendsReceived`, and `cash`.

### Portfolio holdings

The `GET /v1/portfolio/holdings/{symbolQuoteId}` response now includes `currentPrice`, `currentPriceLocal`, `currentExchangeRate`, `unrealisedBoughtShares`, `unrealisedGainsProduct`, `unrealisedGainsProductPercentage`, `unrealisedGainsExchangeRate`, `unrealisedGainsExchangeRatePercentage`, `unrealisedGains`, `unrealisedGainsPercentage`, `realisedGains`, `realisedGainsPercentage`, `totalGains`, `totalGainsPercentage`, `totalBoughtShares`, `totalSoldShares`, `dividendsTax`, `dividendsGains`, `dividendsGainsPercentage`, and `portfolioWeightRelative`.

### Prices endpoint

Historical prices have moved from `GET /v1/symbol-quotes/{symbolQuoteId}/prices` to `GET /v1/prices` with a `symbol_quote_id` query parameter. The old nested endpoint has been removed.

### Date parameters

The `from` and `to` parameters on `GET /v1/portfolio/performance`, `GET /v1/portfolio/gains`, and `GET /v1/portfolio/performance/chart` have been renamed to `dateFrom` and `dateTo` for consistency with other date-filtered endpoints.

### Portfolio performance chart

The `fields` parameter on `GET /v1/portfolio/performance/chart` is now required and must be provided as the first parameter.

### Rate limiting

The rate limit has been increased from 600 to 10,000 requests per minute per authenticated user.

### API documentation

Endpoints are now organized into logical groups (Bookings, Dividends, Portfolio, Symbols, etc.) with descriptive summaries for every endpoint.

## Removed

- `GET /v1/health` — Health check endpoint
- `GET /v1/symbol-quotes/{symbolQuoteId}/prices` — Replaced by `GET /v1/prices`

---

## v0.1.0 (2026-03-17)

## Added

### Authentication

- OAuth2 Authorization Code with PKCE flow for third-party integrations

### Bookings

- `GET /v1/bookings` — List bookings with pagination and filtering
- `GET /v1/bookings/{bookingId}` — Get a specific booking
- `POST /v1/bookings` — Create a new booking
- `PUT /v1/bookings/{bookingId}` — Update a booking
- `DELETE /v1/bookings/{bookingId}` — Delete a booking

### Dividends

- `GET /v1/dividends` — List dividends with pagination and filtering
- `GET /v1/dividends/{dividendId}` — Get a specific dividend
- `POST /v1/dividends` — Create a new dividend
- `PUT /v1/dividends/{dividendId}` — Update a dividend
- `DELETE /v1/dividends/{dividendId}` — Delete a dividend

### Expenses

- `GET /v1/expenses` — List expenses with pagination and filtering
- `GET /v1/expenses/{expenseId}` — Get a specific expense
- `POST /v1/expenses` — Create a new expense
- `PUT /v1/expenses/{expenseId}` — Update an expense
- `DELETE /v1/expenses/{expenseId}` — Delete an expense

### Transactions

- `GET /v1/transactions` — List transactions with pagination and filtering
- `GET /v1/transactions/{transactionId}` — Get a specific transaction
- `POST /v1/transactions` — Create a new transaction
- `PUT /v1/transactions/{transactionId}` — Update a transaction
- `DELETE /v1/transactions/{transactionId}` — Delete a transaction

### Portfolio

- `GET /v1/portfolio` — Get portfolio summary with total value, gain/loss, dividends received, and cash position
- `GET /v1/portfolio/snapshots` — List historical portfolio snapshots
- `GET /v1/portfolio/distributions` — Get portfolio distributions by sector, country, and currency
- `GET /v1/portfolio/holdings/{symbolQuoteId}` — Get a specific holding with quantity, average price, current value, gain/loss, and weight percentage
- `GET /v1/portfolio/performance` — Get portfolio performance metrics over configurable time periods
- `GET /v1/portfolio/holdings/{symbolQuoteId}/dividend-statistics` — Get dividend statistics for a specific holding

### Symbols & Prices

- `GET /v1/symbols/search` — Search symbols by name or ticker
- `GET /v1/symbols/{symbolId}` — Get symbol details
- `GET /v1/symbol-quotes` — List symbol quotes
- `GET /v1/symbol-quotes/{symbolQuoteId}` — Get a specific symbol quote
- `GET /v1/symbol-quotes/{symbolQuoteId}/prices` — List historical prices for a symbol quote

### Brokers

- `GET /v1/brokers` — List available brokers
- `GET /v1/brokers/{brokerId}` — Get broker details

### Investment Strategies

- `GET /v1/investment-strategies` — List investment strategies
- `GET /v1/investment-strategies/{investmentStrategyId}` — Get a specific investment strategy
- `POST /v1/investment-strategies` — Create an investment strategy
- `PUT /v1/investment-strategies/{investmentStrategyId}` — Update an investment strategy
- `DELETE /v1/investment-strategies/{investmentStrategyId}` — Delete an investment strategy
- `POST /v1/investment-strategies/link-symbols` — Link symbols to investment strategies

### Other

- `GET /v1/calendar-events` — List upcoming dividend calendar events
- `GET /v1/exchange-rates` — List exchange rates between currencies
- `GET /v1/x-ray/holdings` — List X-Ray holdings showing underlying ETF/fund constituents
- `GET /v1/health` — Health check endpoint

### API Documentation

- Interactive API documentation available at `/docs/api` powered by Scalar
- Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification at `/docs/api.json`

### Rate Limiting

- API requests are limited to 250 requests per minute per authenticated user

get.description: List OAuth applications that have been granted access to the account. Returns one entry per authorized client with scope and usage information. Useful for reviewing and managing third-party integrations.
Get details for an authorized OAuth application including all individual access tokens with their scopes, creation date, and expiration.

delete.description: Revoke all access for an OAuth application. Revokes all access tokens and refresh tokens for the specified client, effectively disconnecting the integration. The application will need to re-authorize to regain access.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The OAuth client ID to revoke access for.

delete.description: Revoke a single access token. Also revokes the associated refresh token. Other tokens for the same client remain active.

parameters.name: tokenId
parameters.description: The access token ID to revoke.

get.description: Retrieve all published API changelog entries, ordered from newest to oldest.

List corporate actions (stock splits, ISIN changes, exchange changes) with optional filtering by symbol IDs, symbol quote IDs, type, or date range. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

get.description: Retrieve the complete portfolio snapshot including all holdings, cash balance, and financial summary. Returns unrealised gains/losses, realised gains from sold positions, dividend income, transaction costs, and totals. Defaults to the latest snapshot; provide a date for historical view. Essential for portfolio overview.

Retrieve all individual holdings from the portfolio snapshot. Returns each holding with its current price, shares, gains/losses, dividends, and weight. Defaults to the latest snapshot; provide a date for historical view. Use this instead of GetPortfolio when you only need the holdings without the portfolio summary.

List historical portfolio snapshots with financial summaries. Each snapshot represents the portfolio state at a specific date. Filter by date range to analyze portfolio evolution over time. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending. Does not include individual holdings; use GetPortfolio for full details.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter snapshots from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter snapshots up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter snapshots by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

get.description: List upcoming events for securities in the current portfolio. Includes earnings dates and dividend payments. Filter by date range, security, or event type.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter calendar events by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Filter by event types (ex-dividend, payment-dividend, earnings-release, earnings-presentation, earnings-call, earnings-press-conference, sales-and-trading-statement-release, sales-and-trading-statement-call-and-presentation, shareholder-meeting, investor-day, economic, no-events-today).

get.description: Analyze portfolio composition by sector, industry, industry group, country, continent, currency, asset type, market capitalization, exchange, broker, and investment strategy. Returns percentage breakdowns for each category based on current holdings. Useful for diversification analysis and identifying concentration risk.
Calculate total portfolio gains and percentage return for a time period. Returns the net gain/loss amount and percentage using the money-weighted rate of return. Defaults to year-to-date.

List custom investment strategies with optional name search. Results are paginated and include the count of securities linked to each strategy. Useful for organizing portfolio holdings by investment approach.

post.description: Create a custom investment strategy to categorize securities. Strategies help organize holdings by investment thesis (e.g., growth, value, dividend). Each strategy has a name, icon, and color for easy identification. Securities can be linked to strategies to track allocation by strategy.

get.description: Retrieve a single investment strategy by ID. Returns strategy details including name, icon, color, and count of linked securities.

put.description: Modify an existing investment strategy. Updates name, icon, or color. Does not affect linked securities.

delete.description: Permanently remove an investment strategy. All links to securities are removed before deletion. This action cannot be undone.

post.description: Assign securities to investment strategies. Replaces existing strategy assignments for the provided securities. Each security can belong to multiple strategies. Used to categorize holdings by investment thesis for allocation analysis.

parameters.name: links
parameters.description: Array of symbol-strategy links. Each item should have symbolId and investmentStrategyId.

get.description: Calculate portfolio performance metrics for a time period. Returns money-weighted rate of return, total gain/loss, capital growth, and dividend income. Compares portfolio value at period start vs end, accounting for deposits, withdrawals, and transactions. Defaults to year-to-date.

Get portfolio performance as a time series chart. Returns data points grouped by day, week, month, quarter, or year. Each tick contains the requested portfolio metrics (e.g. currentWorth, totalGains, dividendsReceived). Useful for plotting portfolio value, gains, or income over time. Defaults to year-to-date with auto-detected grouping.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Portfolio fields to include per tick.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: Group ticks by period: day, week, month, quarter, year. Auto-detected from date range if omitted.

get.description: Perform look-through analysis of ETF and fund holdings. Reveals the underlying individual stocks owned indirectly through fund positions. Calculates portfolio weight for each underlying holding based on fund allocations. Useful for understanding true diversification and concentration across the entire portfolio. Requires X-Ray feature access.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of holdings to return (default: 100, max: 500).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by holding name, ISIN, or ticker code.

parameters.name: filterBySourceSymbolIds
parameters.description: Filter by specific ETF/fund source symbol IDs.

parameters.name: useSourceWeights
parameters.description: Use source weights instead of portfolio weights (default: false).

get.description: List supported brokerage firms with optional name search. Brokers represent the institutions where portfolio accounts are held. Required when creating transactions, bookings, dividends, and expenses to track which account was affected.
Retrieve a single broker by ID. Returns broker details including name and supported import formats. Used to validate broker references in transactions.

List historical currency exchange rates between two currencies. Filter by exact date or date range. Rates are used to convert foreign currency transactions to the portfolio base currency. Provide 3-letter ISO currency codes (e.g., USD, EUR).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Base currency code (3-letter ISO code, e.g. USD).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Quote currency code (3-letter ISO code, e.g. EUR).

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter exchange rates by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter rates from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter rates up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

get.description: List historical closing prices for a ticker symbol. Filter by exact date or date range. Prices include open, high, low, close, and volume. Useful for analyzing price movements and calculating performance.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter prices by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter prices from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter prices up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

get.description: Fetch prices for up to 50 ticker symbols in a single request. More efficient than individual requests when analyzing multiple securities. Filter by date or date range. When no date filter is provided, returns the latest price for each symbol. Results are grouped by symbol ID.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter prices by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter prices from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter prices up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

get.description: List ticker symbols (quotes) for securities. A symbol quote represents a specific security on a specific exchange in a specific currency. Filter by security ID, ticker code, or exchange. Each quote includes exchange and currency information needed for transactions.

Retrieve a single ticker symbol quote by ID. Returns the security ticker, exchange, and currency information needed for creating transactions. A quote identifies where and in what currency a security trades.

Search the securities database by name, ISIN, or ticker code. Returns matching stocks, ETFs, funds, and other instruments. Results include a flag indicating whether the security is currently in the portfolio. Useful for finding securities to add to transactions or research.
Retrieve a single security by ID. Returns detailed information including name, ISIN, ticker, type (stock, ETF, fund, etc.), and metadata. Use SearchSymbols to find securities by name or ticker.

List cash bookings (deposits, withdrawals, fees, interest) with optional filtering by date range or broker. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending. Useful for tracking cash flows and reconciling broker statements.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter bookings by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter bookings from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter bookings up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

post.description: Record a cash movement against a brokerage account. Bookings track deposits (positive amounts), withdrawals (negative amounts), fees, and interest payments. Updates the portfolio cash position and triggers recalculation. The amount can be positive (deposit) or negative (withdrawal/fee).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The booking date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The booking amount (can be negative for withdrawals). Positive for deposits, negative for withdrawals.

get.description: Retrieve a single cash booking by ID. Returns booking details including date, amount, broker, and booking type.

put.description: Modify an existing cash booking. Updates the portfolio cash position and triggers recalculation. Can change date, amount, or broker assignment.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The booking date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The booking amount (can be negative for withdrawals).

delete.description: Permanently remove a cash booking. Updates the portfolio cash position and recalculates metrics. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: List dividend payments with optional filtering by date range, broker, or security. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending. Useful for tracking income, tax reporting, and analyzing dividend yield across the portfolio.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter dividends by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter dividends from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter dividends up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: symbolQuoteId
parameters.description: Filter dividends by symbol quote ID.

post.description: Record a dividend payment received for a security. Tracks gross amount, tax withheld, and exchange rate. Updates portfolio income totals and triggers performance recalculation. Returns the created dividend with calculated net amount.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The dividend date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

get.description: Retrieve a single dividend by ID. Returns dividend details including security, gross amount, tax, exchange rate, and calculated net amount.

put.description: Modify an existing dividend record. Updates portfolio income totals and recalculates performance metrics. Can change date, amounts, tax, or security.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The dividend date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

delete.description: Permanently remove a dividend record. Updates portfolio income totals and recalculates performance metrics. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: List portfolio expenses with optional filtering by date range, broker, or expense type. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending. Useful for tracking fees, analyzing cost impact, and tax reporting.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter expenses by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter expenses from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter expenses up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

post.description: Record a portfolio-related expense such as broker fees, management fees, or account charges. Expenses reduce portfolio performance and are tracked separately from transaction costs. Updates expense totals and triggers recalculation.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The expense date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

get.description: Retrieve a single expense by ID. Returns expense details including type, amount, broker, date, and description.

put.description: Modify an existing expense record. Updates expense totals and recalculates performance metrics. Can change amount, type, broker, or description.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The expense date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15).

delete.description: Permanently remove an expense record. Updates expense totals and recalculates performance metrics. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: List import history with status and result counts. Results are paginated and ordered by most recent first.

post.description: Upload a broker export file to import transactions, dividends, bookings, and expenses. The file is processed asynchronously. Poll GET /v1/imports/{id} to check the status. Supported formats: CSV, XLSX, TXT, XML. The broker is auto-detected from the file content, or you can hint it with the importer parameter.

parameters.name: importer
parameters.description: Broker importer identifier hint (e.g. "degiro-portfolio-csv", "trading212"). Optional — auto-detected if omitted.

get.description: Get the status and results of an import. Returns the current status (waiting, success, failed) and counts of imported transactions, dividends, bookings, and expenses. Poll this endpoint after creating an import to check when processing completes.
Get the authenticated user's profile including name, email, locale, timezone, currency preference, and account status.

List buy and sell transactions with optional filtering by date range, broker, security, or transaction type. Results are paginated and ordered by date descending (newest first). Useful for reviewing trading activity or generating reports.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Filter transactions by specific dates (array of YYYY-MM-DD strings).

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter transactions from this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter transactions up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive).

parameters.name: symbolId
parameters.description: Filter transactions by symbol ID (all quotes for this symbol).

parameters.name: symbolQuoteId
parameters.description: Filter transactions by symbol quote ID.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by transaction action: buy or sell.

post.description: Record a buy or sell transaction for a security. Updates portfolio holdings, cash position, and triggers performance recalculation. Validates that the broker and exchange exist in the portfolio. May warn about exchange/broker mismatches unless force flag is set. Returns the created transaction with calculated totals.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The transaction date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15). Must be in the past or today.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The price per share with currency, e.g. "EUR 100.00".

parameters.name: costs
parameters.description: Optional transaction costs with currency, e.g. "EUR 5.00".

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force creation despite business rule warnings.

get.description: Retrieve a single transaction by ID. Returns full transaction details including security information, broker, amounts, costs, and tax.

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The ID of the transaction to retrieve.

put.description: Modify an existing buy or sell transaction. Updates portfolio holdings, cash position, and recalculates performance metrics. Validates that the new symbol quote belongs to the same security as the original. May warn about exchange/broker mismatches unless force flag is set.

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The ID of the transaction to update.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The transaction date and time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z or 2026-01-15). Must be in the past or today.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The price per share with currency, e.g. "EUR 100.00".

parameters.name: costs
parameters.description: Optional transaction costs with currency, e.g. "EUR 5.00".

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force update despite business rule warnings.

delete.description: Permanently remove a buy or sell transaction. Updates portfolio holdings, cash position, and recalculates performance metrics. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The ID of the transaction to delete.

get.description:
Start the OAuth2 Authorization Code with PKCE flow. Redirect the user's browser to this endpoint to request access to their account.

**Flow:**
1. Generate a random `code_verifier` (43-128 characters, URL-safe)
2. Derive the `code_challenge` by base64url-encoding the SHA-256 hash of the `code_verifier`
3. Redirect the user to this endpoint with the required parameters
4. The user sees a consent screen and approves or denies access
5. On approval, the user is redirected to your `redirect_uri` with `code` and `state` query parameters

**Scopes:**
| Scope | Access |
|-------|--------|
| `read` | Read portfolio data — bookings, dividends, expenses, transactions, portfolio (default) |
| `write` | Create, update, and delete portfolio data |

Request multiple scopes as a space-separated string (e.g. `read write`). If omitted, only `read` is granted.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The URL to redirect the user to after authorization.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: Must be `code` for the authorization code flow.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Space-separated list of scopes (e.g. `read write`). Defaults to `read` if omitted.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: An opaque value used to prevent CSRF attacks. Returned unchanged in the redirect.

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge derived from the `code_verifier` using S256.

302.description: Redirects to the consent screen, or back to `redirect_uri` with `code` and `state` query parameters upon approval.

post.description:
Exchange an authorization code for an access token, or refresh an existing token.

**Authorization Code Exchange** — After the user approves access at the authorize endpoint, exchange the authorization `code` and your `code_verifier` for tokens. Set `grant_type` to `authorization_code`.

**Refresh Token** — When the access token expires, use the `refresh_token` to obtain a new pair without user interaction. Set `grant_type` to `refresh_token`.

**Token Lifetimes:**
| Token | Lifetime |
|-------|----------|
| Access token | 1 hour |
| Refresh token | 30 days |

**Usage:** Include the access token in all API requests as a Bearer token:
```
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}
```

**Rate Limiting:** API requests are limited to 250 requests per minute per authenticated user.

redirect_uri.description: Must match the `redirect_uri` used in the authorization request.

code.description: The authorization code received from the authorize endpoint.

code_verifier.description: The original PKCE code verifier string.

access_token.description: The access token to use in API requests.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token to obtain new access tokens.

400.description: Invalid request — the authorization code is invalid, expired, or the code verifier does not match.

scopes.read: Read portfolio data (bookings, dividends, expenses, transactions, portfolio)
scopes.write: Create, update, and delete portfolio data
